content.question:
Finish the following sentence without changing the meaning of the sentence printed before it.

1. It's weeks since I last went to the countryside.
👉 I haven't ___
content.answer: been to the countryside for weeks. || been to the countryside for weeks / I haven't been to the countryside for weeks. / I haven't been to the countryside for weeks / I have not been to the countryside for weeks. / I have not been to the countryside for weeks

content.question:
Finish the following sentence without changing the meaning of the sentence printed before it.

2. Our school has never held a Christmas party before.
👉 It's ___
content.answer: the first time our school has held a Christmas party. / the first time our school has ever held a Christmas party. || the first time our school has held a Christmas party / the first time our school has ever held a Christmas party / It's the first time our school has held a Christmas party. / It's the first time our school has held a Christmas party / It is the first time our school has held a Christmas party. / It is the first time our school has held a Christmas party / It's the first time our school has ever held a Christmas party. / It is the first time our school has ever held a Christmas party.

content.question:
Finish the following sentence without changing the meaning of the sentence printed before it.

3. They began building the tunnel eighteen months ago.
👉 They have ___
content.answer: been building the tunnel for eighteen months. || been building the tunnel for eighteen months / They have been building the tunnel for eighteen months. / They have been building the tunnel for eighteen months / They've been building the tunnel for eighteen months. / They've been building the tunnel for eighteen months

content.question:
Finish the following sentence without changing the meaning of the sentence printed before it.

4. When was the planet Pluto discovered?
👉 How ___
content.answer: long ago was the planet Pluto discovered? / long is it since the planet Pluto was discovered? || long ago was the planet Pluto discovered / How long ago was the planet Pluto discovered? / How long ago was the planet Pluto discovered / long is it since the planet Pluto was discovered / How long is it since the planet Pluto was discovered? / How long is it since the planet Pluto was discovered

content.question:
Finish the following sentence without changing the meaning of the sentence printed before it.

5. He has never felt so embarrassed before.
👉 It's ___
content.answer: the first time he has felt so embarrassed. / the first time he has ever felt so embarrassed. || the first time he has felt so embarrassed / the first time he has ever felt so embarrassed / It's the first time he has felt so embarrassed. / It's the first time he has felt so embarrassed / It is the first time he has felt so embarrassed. / It is the first time he has felt so embarrassed / It's the first time he has ever felt so embarrassed. / It is the first time he has ever felt so embarrassed.

content.question:
Finish the following sentence without changing the meaning of the sentence printed before it.

6. They raided the fridge as soon as their mother had left.
👉 They didn't ___
content.answer: raid the fridge until their mother had left. / start raiding the fridge until their mother had left. || raid the fridge until the moment their mother had left. / raid the fridge until their mother had left / raid the fridge until the moment their mother had left / start raiding the fridge until their mother had left / They didn't raid the fridge until their mother had left. / They didn't raid the fridge until their mother had left / They did not raid the fridge until their mother had left. / They did not raid the fridge until their mother had left

content.question:
Finish the following sentence without changing the meaning of the sentence printed before it.

7. I've never met such an immature person!
👉 She's the ___
content.answer: most immature person I've ever met! || most immature person I've ever met / most immature person I have ever met! / most immature person I have ever met / She's the most immature person I've ever met! / She's the most immature person I've ever met / She is the most immature person I've ever met! / She is the most immature person I've ever met / She is the most immature person I have ever met! / She is the most immature person I have ever met

content.question:
Finish the following sentence without changing the meaning of the sentence printed before it.

8. France hasn't won a gold medal in this sport for a long time.
👉 It's a ___
content.answer: long time since France won a gold medal in this sport. || long time since France won a gold medal in this sport / It's a long time since France won a gold medal in this sport. / It's a long time since France won a gold medal in this sport / It is a long time since France won a gold medal in this sport. / It is a long time since France won a gold medal in this sport

content.question:
Finish the following sentence without changing the meaning of the sentence printed before it.

9. I started studying law two years ago.
👉 I have ___
content.answer: been studying law for two years. || been studying law for two years / I have been studying law for two years. / I have been studying law for two years / I've been studying law for two years. / I've been studying law for two years

content.question:
Finish the following sentence without changing the meaning of the sentence printed before it.

10. How long ago did you realise the truth?
👉 How long is it ___
content.answer: since you realised the truth? || since you realised the truth / How long is it since you realised the truth? / How long is it since you realised the truth

content.question:
Finish the following sentence without changing the meaning of the sentence printed before it.

11. The doctor didn't start the operation until the anaesthetic had taken effect.
👉 The doctor waited ___
content.answer: until the anaesthetic had taken effect before starting the operation. / until the anaesthetic had taken effect before he started the operation. || until the anaesthetic had taken effect before starting the operation / until the anaesthetic had taken effect before he started the operation / The doctor waited until the anaesthetic had taken effect before starting the operation. / The doctor waited until the anaesthetic had taken effect before starting the operation / The doctor waited until the anaesthetic had taken effect before he started the operation.

content.question:
Finish the following sentence without changing the meaning of the sentence printed before it.

12. He realised his mistake when it was too late.
👉 He didn't ___
content.answer: realise his mistake until it was too late. || realise his mistake until it was too late / He didn't realise his mistake until it was too late. / He didn't realise his mistake until it was too late / He did not realise his mistake until it was too late. / He did not realise his mistake until it was too late

content.question:
Finish the following sentence without changing the meaning of the sentence printed before it.

13. It's the most ridiculous suggestion I've ever heard!
👉 I've never ___
content.answer: heard such a ridiculous suggestion! || heard such a ridiculous suggestion / I've never heard such a ridiculous suggestion! / I've never heard such a ridiculous suggestion / I have never heard such a ridiculous suggestion! / I have never heard such a ridiculous suggestion

content.question:
Finish the following sentence without changing the meaning of the sentence printed before it.

14. When did you last see Paul?
👉 When was ___
content.answer: the last time you saw Paul? || the last time you saw Paul / When was the last time you saw Paul? / When was the last time you saw Paul

content.question:
Finish the following sentence without changing the meaning of the sentence printed before it.

15. After he had finished his homework, he went for a walk.
👉 Having ___
content.answer: finished his homework, he went for a walk. || finished his homework, he went for a walk / Having finished his homework, he went for a walk. / Having finished his homework, he went for a walk

content.question:
Write a new sentence as similar as possible in meaning to the original sentence but using the word given. The word must not be altered in any way.

16. I believe it's going to rain.
content.answer: I have a feeling it's going to rain. || I have a feeling it's going to rain / I have a feeling it is going to rain. / I have a feeling it is going to rain

content.question:
Write a new sentence as similar as possible in meaning to the original sentence but using the word given. The word must not be altered in any way.

17. I haven't been to the cinema for weeks.
content.answer: The last time I went to the cinema was weeks ago. / I haven't been to the cinema for quite some time. / I haven't been to the cinema for a long time. || The last time I went to the cinema was weeks ago / I haven't been to the cinema for quite some time / I have not been to the cinema for quite some time. / I have not been to the cinema for quite some time / I haven't been to the cinema for a long time / I have not been to the cinema for a long time. / I have not been to the cinema for a long time

content.question:
Write a new sentence as similar as possible in meaning to the original sentence but using the word given. The word must not be altered in any way.

18. The contest takes place tomorrow.
content.answer: The contest is being held tomorrow. / The contest will be held tomorrow. / The contest is going to be held tomorrow. || The contest is being held tomorrow / The contest will be held tomorrow / The contest is going to be held tomorrow

content.question:
Write a new sentence as similar as possible in meaning to the original sentence but using the word given. The word must not be altered in any way.

19. She started cleaning as soon as the children had left.
content.answer: Not until the children had left did she start cleaning. / She didn't start cleaning until the children had left. / It wasn't until the children had left that she started cleaning. / She waited until the children had left before she started cleaning. || Not until the children had left did she start cleaning / She didn't start cleaning until the children had left / She did not start cleaning until the children had left. / She did not start cleaning until the children had left / It wasn't until the children had left that she started cleaning / It was not until the children had left that she started cleaning. / It was not until the children had left that she started cleaning / She waited until the children had left before she started cleaning

content.question:
Write a new sentence as similar as possible in meaning to the original sentence but using the word given. The word must not be altered in any way.

20. It's certain that they'll be late.
content.answer: They're bound to be late. || They're bound to be late / They are bound to be late. / They are bound to be late

content.question:
Write a new sentence as similar as possible in meaning to the original sentence but using the word given. The word must not be altered in any way.

21. Was her call answered by anyone?
content.answer: Was there any answer to her call? / Did anyone answer her call? || Was there any answer to her call / Did anyone answer her call
